title: cynanchum sublanceolatum var. auriculatum
intro: Cynanchum sublanceolatum var. auriculatum, also known as the auriculated wolf's milk, is a plant species within the Apocynaceae family, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is traditionally valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant effects, derived from its bioactive compounds such as alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Cynanchum sublanceolatum var. auriculatum include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in treating fever, pain, and digestive problems. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared through infusions, decoctions, or extracts, which can be administered orally or used topically, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
